full_text:
t-CyCIF protocol 
 Day 0 
 Preparations 
 ● Make sure that the tissue section being stained is not older than 6 months and has been stored at 
 +4 0 C. 
 ● Set the oven’s temperature to 56 0 C (double check the temperature using a thermometer). 
 ● Check the levels of xylene and alcohol in the dewaxing and rehydration series in the fume hood, if 
 the levels are too low add more xylene or respective alcohol. Prepare fresh xylene and alcohol 
 series after using them 5 times. 
 ● Make sure that all the buffers and antibodies are available: antigen retrieval (100mM citric acid pH 
 6.0), odyssey blocking buffer (not available in market anymore). Use ,superblock 1 litre thermofisher 
 ref: 37515 instead. Never use an intercept blocking buffer from licor that was supposed to be the 
 replacement for the Odyssey buffer. 
 Day 1 (approx. 6.5 h) 
 1. Dewaxing and antigen retrieval (approx. 2h 10 min) 
 Baking: place the slides vertically into a grey plastic slide holder and bake in a 56 0 C oven for 30 min to 1h. 
 Note: If the slides are coated with wax for long-term storage then keep it longer in Xylene. Start with 10 
 mins 3X times Xylene instead of 5 mins 3 X xylene. If you still see wax remaining in the slide, dip it for 10 
 more minutes in Xylene 
 1.1. Deparaffinization + rehydration: 
 3 x 5 min Xylene 
 1 x 3 min 100% ethanol 
 1 x 3 min 90% ethanol

see wax remaining in the slide, dip it for 10 
 more minutes in Xylene 
 1.1. Deparaffinization + rehydration: 
 3 x 5 min Xylene 
 1 x 3 min 100% ethanol 
 1 x 3 min 90% ethanol 
 1 x 3 min 70% ethanol 
 1 x 3 min 50% ethanol 
 1 x 3 min 30% ethanol 
 2 x 1 min successive 1x PBS solution 
 1

1.2. Antigen retrieval: 
 Always use freshly prepared citric acid buffer, 10 mM, pH 6.0: 
 From 100mM stock 🡪 25mL of stock solution + 225mL of MQ. 
 100 mM citric acid 1L: 
 ● Dissolve 21.0 grams of citric acid monohydrate (Sigma #C1909) into 800 mL of MQ H20. 
 ● Add 9.94 grams of NaOH pellets (Merck # 1310-73-2) 
 ● Leave on a magnetic stirrer to thoroughly mi 
 ● Measure the pH and set it to 6.0 
 ● Level the volume up to 1L with MQ H20. 
 Note: Use the solution not more than 6 months after preparation. Transfer the slides onto a 
 white plastic holder and in a glass dish - fill with 250 mL citric acid buffer. 
 Use the New ANTIGEN RETRIEVAL CHAMBER (ARC): 
 ● Add 550 ml of MQ water to the chamber. Inside the chamber are 3 rack holders, add 
 about 225 ml Citric acid buffer to the rack holder where you intend to place slides, 
 add MQ water if you don’t intend to place slides. 
 ● Now place the slides in the rack holder containing Citric acid buffer.Tighten the lid 
 firmly then turn the device on and select preset settings from saved protocol of 95 0 C 
 without preheat.Which is the 3rd row in saved protocols.The total time takes around 
 45 minutes. 
 ● After the protocol is over, open the lid carefully(use heat resistance gloves) and 
 transfer the slides and citric acid buffer to a new plastic rack holder. Take it to the 
 hood to cool it faster.

is over, open the lid carefully(use heat resistance gloves) and 
 transfer the slides and citric acid buffer to a new plastic rack holder. Take it to the 
 hood to cool it faster. 20 minutes should be enough for the temperature to cool down 
 to 40 0 C. 
 ● Once the slides have cooled down, wash three times with 1x PBS, ~ 3 min each. 
 OR 
 Old protocol for antigen retrieval. 
 ● Boil sections in a microwave at 600 W as follows: 
 ● Boil for 4 min - leave the slides inside for 5 min. 
 ● Boil for 2 min - leave the slides inside for 5 min. 
 ● Boil for 2 min. 
 ● Let to cool down for 20 mins RT 
 ● Wash three times with 1x PBS, ~ 3 min each. 
 2

2. Pre-staining (approx. 2h) 
 Note: Odyssey buffer should be filtered with 0.22uM filter each time we make an aliquot. 
 2.1. Prepare a moist incubation chamber to minimize evaporation: Wet regular tissue paper under the tap 
 water and fold it to fit inside the grey slide box. After cooling and washing from step 1.2., take out the 
 slides one at a time, dry the surroundings of the tissue section using soft fiber-free tissue paper. 
 Without letting the tissue dry, quickly add 200-300 uL Odyssey blocking buffer per section and 
 incubate for 30 minutes at Room Temperature. 
 Note: The use of PAP pen is not needed. Do not spread the blocking buffer with a pipette tip, as 
 you might scratch the tissue while doing so . In addition, thorough drying of the surroundings of the 
 tissue creates a hydrophobic surface so that the solution does not spread outside the tissue. 
 2.2. After the incubation, remove the blocking buffer by vertically tapping the slide on a tissue paper. Next, 
 pre-stain the slides with secondary antibodies and Hoechst only by adding 200-300uL of pre-diluted 
 antibodies per section. Incubate for 60 min at RT in a moist chamber (from 2.1.) . 
 Prepare a cocktail of secondary antibodies and Hoechst in Odyssey blocking buffer: 
 488 goat anti-rabbit 1:1000 cat:A11034 Slot:+4C Box1 C2 
 555 goat anti-rat 1:1000 cat:A21434 Slot:+4C Box1 C3

l of secondary antibodies and Hoechst in Odyssey blocking buffer: 
 488 goat anti-rabbit 1:1000 cat:A11034 Slot:+4C Box1 C2 
 555 goat anti-rat 1:1000 cat:A21434 Slot:+4C Box1 C3 
 647 goat anti-mouse 1:1000 cat:A21235 Slot:+4C Box1 C4 
 HOECHST 1:5000 cat:H3570 Slot:+4C Box1 I9 
 Note: HOECHST is usually diluted from pre-diluted 1:100 in Odyssey buffer and used for about a 
 month Dilute 1:50 from 1:100 to get final dilution of 1:5000.Hoechst stock in +4 Fridge door. 
 2.3. Transfer the slides into a vertical grey plastic holder placed in a white plastic container with 1xPBS. 
 Wash three times with 1x PBS, ~ 3 min each. 
 3. Bleaching/Fluorophore inactivation (approx. 2h) 
 3.1. Prepare 50 mL bleaching solution for 8 slides in a falcon tube as follows: 
 40 ml 1xPBS 
 7.5ml 30% H2O2 
 2.5ml 480 mM NaOH 
 3.1. Pour the bleaching solution into a white slide box lid placed on top of an LED light. 
 3

3.2. Using forceps, transfer the slides into a bleaching solution; make sure that the slides are completely 
 immersed in the solution. Place another LED light on top of the slide box (step 3.2). Connect bottom 
 and top LED lights to power. 
 3.3. Photo bleach the sides for 70 min at RT, during which change the buffer solution after every 20 min. 
 NOTE: Only for cycles 0 The rest of the cycles are only 1x after 25 min. 
 3.4. Wash four times in PBS, ~ 3 min each. 
 4. Staining with primary antibodies 
 4.1. Stain with unconjugated primary antibodies o/n at +4 ° C diluted in Odyssey solution. If you have 
 conjugated different species to the unconjugated then add at the same time. 
 Day 2 
 5. Staining with secondary antibodies 
 5.1. Wash three times with 1x PBS, ~ 3 min each. 
 Incubate secondary antibodies 1:2000 and Hoechst 1:10000 in Odyssey buffer for 1 hr RT. 
 5.2. Wash three times with 1x PBS x3, ~ 3 min each. 
 5.3. Add Conjugated antibody if it’s from the same host species as another primary-secondary antibody 
 combination , incubate for 1 hour RT. Then wash with 1X PBS 3 times. 
 5.4. Dry the surroundings of the tissue section using soft fiber-free tissue paper. Add 80-100 uL of 70% 
 glycerol in PBS per section and carefully cover with a coverslip, avoiding air bubbles. 
 5.5 Scan the slides. 
 4 Slide ID 1’ Ab /Conjugated

Note: Volume of the 70% glycerol depends on the size of the tissue, bigger tissues may need more 
 than 100ul for example 1cm x 1cm tissue might need 120 ul of glycerol 
 6. Cyclic phase – repeat steps as before 
 6.1. Decoverslip using 1x PBS 
 6.2. Photobleach for 50 mins, change the solution after 25 mins. 
 6.3. Wash four times in PBS, ~ 3 min each. 
 6.4. Stain with pre-conjugated antibodies and Hoechst 1:10000 o/n at +4°C. 
 6.5. Wash three times with 1x PBS, ~ 3 min each. 
 6.6. Mount with 70% glycerol and scan 
 5
